name: commons-data-hypergraph
horizon: data-model + seed audit. Verify substrate-honesty for v1+v2 claims, close seed coverage gaps for the 9 product-meaningful unseeded tables, reduce schema drift, add integration tests proving the substrate works end-to-end.
premise: Two capability hypergraphs (v1, v2) shipped 73 tasks of substrate. This hypergraph is the foundation audit — does the substrate actually do what its code claims? Are seed/test environments coherent with shipped substrate? Are there orphaned schema fields the migration left behind? No new feature work; reductive + verifying.

node_types.description: Carry-over context from v1/v2 capability clusters. NOT the realization metric here — this hypergraph addresses substrate honesty within already-claimed clusters.
Four execution tracks: verify substrate · seed coverage · schema drift · test fixtures.
Three waves — verify before seed (so seed targets real gaps), drift before tests (so tests don't bake in dead fields).
Concrete data/seed/test unit. Most tasks are S-tier; the audit work is reading code, the seed work is appending to seed.ts, the tests are vitest specs.
None in this hypergraph — no ops gates. All engineering-bound.

edge_types.description: Source must close before target can start.
Source task addresses uncertainty in a v1/v2 cluster claim. Many-to-many.
Source verifies the honesty of a v1/v2 substrate claim. New edge type vs previous hypergraphs — used to track the audit linkage.
Tasks executable concurrently in the same wave.

completion_signal: ALL tasks status ∈ {completed, deferred} AND every verify task either (a) confirms substrate is honest with a code-grounded note, or (b) discovers a gap and adds a fix task to W-1.
